note: filenames keyed to CANONICAL sheet row; 'serves' lists reuse placements; wiring = orchestrator #108

entries.what: Zn/Cu galvanic cell (beakers, electrodes, wire+voltmeter 1.10 V, KNO3 salt bridge) with 4 blank '?' boxes: electrode roles, salt-bridge function, e- direction slot
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no anode/cathode text, no electron-flow or ion-flow arrows, no +/- terminal marks; only prompt-given facts drawn (metals, solutions, 1.10 V, KNO3)

entries.what: Before/After particulate panels for N2+3H2->2NH3: Before drawn with 4 N2 + 12 H2; After panel empty dashed (student draws the 50/75/100%-yield state)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: After panel contains zero particles; no NH3 drawn anywhere; no leftover counts hinted

entries.what: Before/After particulate panels for 2H2+O2->2H2O: Before drawn with 3 H2 + 2 O2; After empty dashed (student identifies limiting reactant and draws products + leftover)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: After panel empty; limiting reactant not marked; no H2O drawn

entries.what: Before/After panels: After drawn with 2 NH3 + 2 leftover H2 (as given in cell); Before empty dashed (student works backwards to initial N2/H2 counts)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: Before panel empty; initial counts (the answer) appear nowhere; After contents are verbatim the given state

entries.what: C2H4 skeletal framework: C-C single line + 4 C-H bonds, atom letters only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no double bond, no electron dots/lone pairs, neutral 4-corner H placement (does not telegraph sp2 planarity answer)

entries.what: Filtration apparatus (stand, ring, funnel + filter paper with solid dots, conical flask with filtrate) with 2 blank '?' boxes at paper+solid and liquid-in-flask
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no 'residue'/'filtrate' text; no equipment names either (keeps asset reusable for row 146 name-the-equipment)

entries.what: Cl mass spectrum: peaks at m/z 35 (75%) and 37 (25%), y scale 0-100, tick numbers shown; axis-label positions are blank '?' boxes
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no 'm/z' / 'relative abundance' axis text (that IS the task); peaks carry no isotope labels

entries.what: Partial Br mass spectrum: peak at m/z 79 (~50%) drawn, axes labeled (given), tick mark at 81 with empty space
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no peak, ghost, or height hint at m/z 81 (drawing it is the exercise)

entries.what: Blank mass-spectrum axes (m/z vs relative abundance 0-100) with ticks at 35 and 37
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no peaks drawn; 3:1 ratio appears nowhere

entries.what: Stoichiometric graph: g NH3 produced vs g N2 added, linear rise then plateau
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no marker/label at the kink, no region annotations (locating them is the task); axis labels are given in cell text

entries.what: Data table: Mg added 1.0-4.0 g vs MgO formed 1.66/3.32/4.98/4.98 g
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: plateau rows styled identically to linear rows (spotting the flattening is the task); no limiting-reactant annotation

entries.what: Blank potential-energy vs reaction-coordinate axes
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no levels, humps, or dH arrows; endo/exo direction not hinted

entries.what: Blank concentration-time axes for N2+3H2<=>2NH3 with dashed stressor markers at t=30 s (N2 added) and t=60 s (T increased, dH<0) - all given in cell
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no concentration curves before or after stressors; no arrows or shift-direction cues

entries.what: Concentration-time axes with given starting points [N2]0=1.00, [H2]0=3.00, [NH3]0=0 and dashed guide at [NH3]eq=0.40 M; no curves
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: consumed amounts (0.20/0.60 M) and final [N2]/[H2] guides NOT drawn - computing/drawing them is the task

entries.what: Blank M-B axes: x = molecular energy, y = fraction of molecules (labels given in cell text)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no curves; T1/T2 peak positions not hinted

entries.what: Maxwell-Boltzmann energy-distribution curve with dashed vertical at the peak and a second dashed line slightly right; 4 blank '?' boxes for x-axis, y-axis, and the two lines
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no 'kinetic energy'/'number of particles'/'most probable'/'average' text anywhere; the average-KE line sits right of the peak per cell text

entries.what: 4-box circular-flow skeleton (Households/Firms/Product market/Factor market) with paired connector lines, arrowheads omitted
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no arrowheads, no flow labels, no teal/amber money-vs-real colour coding (assigning direction + flows IS the exercise)

entries.what: P-Q axes with original demand curve D1 only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no D2, no shift arrows, no direction hint; drawing the shifted curve is the exercise

entries.what: P-Q axes with original supply curve S1 only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no S2, no shift arrows, no direction hint

entries.what: Blank Price/Quantity axes (family asset for the econ draw-a-market cue corpus)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: axes + given labels only

entries.what: Blank two-good PPC axes (generic Good X / Good Y so one asset serves every PPC cue)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no curve, no intercepts, no bow direction

entries.what: PES spectrum with 3 unlabeled peaks (heights 2:2:3, decreasing-binding-energy layout); dashed '?' boxes at both axis-label positions and above each peak
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no subshell labels, no binding-energy numbers, no axis text ('label each peak with its subshell... label the axes' IS the task); left=high-BE ordering is convention the student must apply, not printed

entries.what: PES spectrum with peaks at 2.48/0.63/0.18 MJ/mol (verbatim cell data), heights 2:2:3 readable off a 1-2-3 y-scale; axes labeled binding energy (MJ/mol) / number of electrons
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no 1s/2s/2p labels on peaks, no electron-count callouts (reading heights off the scale IS the task); BE values + axis labels are given in cell text

entries.what: CH3OH sigma skeleton: H3C-O-H connectivity as single lines, atom letters only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no lone pairs on O (adding the 2 lone pairs completes the Lewis structure); all bonds single as in the answer, so connectivity given = formula given

entries.what: CH3COOH sigma skeleton: CH3-C with one O up and one O-H right, ALL bonds single lines, atom letters only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no C=O double bond (both C-O identical single lines, so which O takes the pi bond is not telegraphed beyond the H placement the formula itself gives); zero lone pairs drawn

entries.what: Ethanol (CH3CH2OH) sigma skeleton: C-C-O-H chain + 5 C-H, single lines, atom letters only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no lone pairs on O; electron bookkeeping from the cell text stays in the cell, not on the figure

entries.what: Acetone (CH3COCH3) sigma skeleton: C-C-C chain, central C-O single line up, 6 C-H, atom letters only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: C-O drawn single (adding the pi bond is the exercise); no lone pairs on O; methyl H count is formula-given

entries.what: Before/After panels: After drawn with 4 NH3 and 0 leftover reactants (verbatim cell state); Before empty dashed (student works backwards to 2 N2 + 6 H2)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: Before panel empty; initial N2/H2 counts (the answer) appear nowhere; After contents verbatim the given state

entries.what: Five-sector circular-flow skeleton: u_74 core (Households/Firms/Product market/Factor market, paired connector lines) + Government/Financial sector/Foreign sector centre boxes with bare dashed connector stubs
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no arrowheads, no flow labels, no leakage/injection markings (savings/taxes/imports vs borrowing/G-spending/exports = the M80/M81 exercises), no colour coding; sector names are given in the cues

entries.what: P-Q axes with demand (indigo, D) and supply (amber, S) both drawn and named; intersection deliberately unmarked
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no equilibrium dot, no Pe/Qe labels, no dashed drop lines (labeling the intersection / adding price lines and surplus-shortage marks IS the exercise family)

entries.what: Distillation apparatus: heated round-bottom flask with liquid, thermometer in vapour path, sloped condenser with water jacket, conical collection flask with liquid; 5 dashed '?' boxes with leader lines (flask, heat source, thermometer, condenser, collection flask)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no equipment names (row 154 name-the-5-pieces stays intact), no distillate/residue text, no boiling points, no which-component-first hint

entries.what: 5-option particle-classification substrate: (A) identical spheres far apart, (B) hetero-colour bonded pairs far apart, (C) same-colour pairs + lone spheres mixed, (D) alternating big/small lattice, (E) before->after rearrangement mini-panels; legend only says each colour = a different element
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no state names, no element/compound/mixture verdicts, no physical/chemical labels; panel letters (A)-(E) match the cell text; spacing/arrangement encodes the GIVEN states only

entries.what: Blank pH-vs-volume-of-titrant axes: pH ticks 0/7/14 with dashed neutral gridline at 7 only
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no curves, no equivalence or half-equivalence marks, no buffer-region shading; initial-pH and steepness differences (the Ka-comparison answers) not hinted

entries.what: Bare heating-curve axes with dashed '?' boxes at both axis-label slots (cell asks the student to label heat-added / temperature)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no plateaus drawn, no temperature ticks (0/100 marks would leak plateau heights), no segment count hinted

entries.what: Pressure-time axes with given starting dots P(N2)0=0.50, P(H2)0=1.50, P(NH3)0=0 atm (verbatim cell) on a 0-2.00 atm scale; species dots colour-keyed and named
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no curves, no equilibrium-value guides, no total-pressure direction hint (4mol->2mol reasoning is the question)

entries.what: Blank [A]-vs-time axes, labels given verbatim from the cell hook
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no curves; which curve drops faster (the answer) not hinted

entries.what: Blank Laffer axes: tax rate (0%-100% anchors) vs tax revenue, labels given in cue
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no curve, no peak marker, no rising/falling-portion labels, no Point A (drawing the inverted-U and placing A are the exercises)

entries.what: Gridded axes for the step-demand exercise: quantity ticks 1-6, price ticks 0.10-0.60 (all values verbatim from the cue), full light gridlines
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no steps/staircase drawn, no willingness-to-pay pairing of heights to quantities (the mapping IS the exercise; tick values alone are cue-given)

entries.what: 2x2 goods-classification matrix with cue-given captions (Rival/Non-rival columns, Excludable/Non-excludable rows); all four cells empty
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no goods placed, no category names (private/public/club/common) printed - placing national defense, sandwich, park, toll road, pizza IS the exercise

entries.what: Blank 2-column sorting table: dashed '?' header boxes + 4 empty rows (family asset for every 2-way sort cue)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: headers blank (writing need/want, goods/services etc. is part of each exercise); no items pre-placed

entries.what: Blank 4-column resource-classification matrix: dashed '?' header boxes + 3 empty rows; also the pad for the M12/M13 four-box production-input cues (same blank 4-category scaffold)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: headers blank (land/labor/capital/entrepreneurship are the answer categories); river/teacher/factory/founder not pre-placed

entries.what: Table-to-graph conversion scaffold: empty 4-column demand table (P / Qd A / Qd B / Market headers, structure cue-given) -> arrow -> blank mini P-Q axes
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no quantities, no summed market column values, no demand curve (horizontal summation is the exercise)

entries.what: Two-good opportunity-cost table skeleton: generic Good X / Good Y rows, 'to gain 1 unit / you give up' columns, cost cells empty
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no cost values, no reciprocal (1/x) relationship shown - demonstrating it is the exercise; goods kept generic

entries.what: Import-side world-price underlay: S+D named, dashed P_w line below equilibrium (P_w label only); family asset for the import/tariff/quota cluster; multi-panel cues (243/245/258) use it as the import panel and pair with u_241 for the export panel
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no Qs/Qd droplines, no import bracket, no equilibrium marks, no P_w+T tariff line, no surplus shading - every one of those is a cue exercise; P_w-below-equilibrium is the given import premise

entries.what: Export-side world-price underlay: S+D named, dashed P_w line above equilibrium (P_w label only); export panel partner of u_239 for multi-panel cues
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no Qs/Qd droplines, no export bracket, no producer/consumer-surplus shading (shading and bracketing are the cue exercises); P_w-above-equilibrium is the given export premise

entries.what: Athletic Country PPC scaffold: empty Point/Bats/Rackets schedule table (rows A-F, letters cue-given) + axes labeled bats (x) / rackets (y)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no output values (full A-F schedule is not in the Sheet, so none were fabricated - X86 only reveals B and C), no curve, no plotted points; students bring the schedule from the lesson material

entries.what: H2O heating curve shown-state: 5 segments (-20C solid rise, 0C melt plateau, liquid rise, long 100C boil plateau, gas rise); plateau temperatures readable off y-ticks -20/0/100
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no segment names, no melting/boiling/freezing labels, no dHfus/dHvap values on the figure (identifying and naming segments is the 873/1530 task; 1534 reads the temps off the axis - that reading IS its task)

entries.what: H2O cooling curve shown-state: gas fall from 120C, condensation plateau at 100C, liquid fall, freezing plateau at 0C, solid fall to -20C; temps readable off y-ticks
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no transition names (freezing/condensation are the answers being read), no segment labels

entries.what: KNO3 solubility-vs-temperature curve on full 20-degree/40-gram grid, real curve data (13@0, 32@20, 64@40, 85@50, 106@60, 170@80) consistent with every value the cells quote
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no point markers or callouts at the queried temperatures (reading 20/40/50/60/80 values off the grid IS the task), no saturated/unsaturated zone shading, no crystallization annotation for the 60->20 cooling question

entries.what: Single-step PE profile shown-state: reactants 50 kJ, transition state 130 kJ, products 20 kJ, all readable off gridlined y-ticks (values verbatim from cells)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no dH/Ea(fwd)/Ea(rev) arrows or labels, no exo/endo annotation (labeling those on the diagram is exactly the 1054/1055 task set)

entries.what: Two-step PE profile shown-state: react 0 -> TS1 60 -> intermediate 30 -> TS2 120 -> products -20 kJ (verbatim cell values, readable off gridlined y-ticks)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no rate-determining-step marker, no Ea arrows, no exo/endo verdict, humps and valley unlabeled (identifying step 1, the intermediate, and the RDS are the 1044/1058/1063 tasks)

entries.what: Generic three-step PE profile: 3 humps, 2 valleys, products below reactants; no numeric scale
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: humps/valleys uncounted and unlabeled, no step or intermediate annotations (counting them is the exercise)

entries.what: Strong acid-strong base titration curve shown-state (25.00 mL 0.100 M HCl vs 0.100 M NaOH): starts pH 1.0, symmetric steep jump through pH 7 at 25.00 mL, plateau ~12.5; mL and pH scales readable
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no equivalence-point dot, no dashed guides, no region labels (reading initial pH, locating equivalence volume/pH, and identifying the steep zone are the 1351/1353/1356/1419 tasks)

entries.what: Weak acid-strong base titration curve shown-state (25.00 mL 0.100 M CH3COOH vs 0.100 M NaOH): start pH 2.9, buffer flattening near pH 4.74 around 12.5 mL, equivalence at 25.00 mL pH 8.7, gentler S than SA-SB
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no half-equivalence marker, no pKa dashed line, no buffer-region bracket, no equivalence dot (reading initial pH, spotting pH=pKa at half-equivalence, and explaining equivalence>7 are the tasks); curve geometry itself is the given

entries.what: Weak base-strong acid titration curve shown-state (25.00 mL 0.100 M NH3 vs 0.100 M HCl): start pH 11.1, buffer flattening near pH 9.26 around 12.5 mL, equivalence at 25.00 mL pH 5.3, inverted S
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no half-equivalence marker or 14-pKb line, no equivalence dot, no buffer bracket (reading those features is the 1382/1388 task set)

entries.what: Generic A<=>B approach-to-equilibrium: [A] decays from high (steep then flattening), [B] rises from 0, both plateau at the same time; curves colour-keyed and labeled [A]/[B]
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no t_eq marker or dashed vertical (identifying the moment equilibrium is reached IS the task), no forward/reverse-rate annotations

entries.what: N2+3H2<=>2NH3 approach-to-equilibrium: starts at the verbatim cell values (0.50/1.50/0 M), plateaus at 0.30/0.90/0.40 M so the consumption ratio is exactly 1:3:2; species colour-keyed and named
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no t_eq marker (marking it IS the task), no rate-comparison annotations; plateau values are internally consistent extras, not quoted answers

entries.what: H2+I2<=>2HI approach-to-equilibrium: [H2]=[I2] drawn as one overlapping curve 0.100->0.022 M, [HI] 0->0.156 M (verbatim cell values), plateaus approximately readable off 0.05-gridded scale
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no Kc value or calculation shown, no dashed plateau guides (reading the equilibrium concentrations and computing Kc = [HI]^2/([H2][I2]) IS the task)

entries.what: Overlay shown-state: SA-SB curve (indigo solid, start pH 1.0, equivalence 7) and WA-SB curve (rose dashed, start pH 2.9, buffer flattening, equivalence 8.7) on one grid; curves named strong acid / weak acid (identities given in cell)
entries.answer_leak_check: PASS: no callouts on the four distinguishing features (higher initial pH, buffer region, pH=pKa at half-equivalence, equivalence>7) - listing them from the comparison IS the task
